name: 郭孔丰

education.institution: National University of Singapore (Desautels Faculty of Management)

person_relationships.description: Nephew of Malaysian Chinese billionaire Robert Kuok; merged Kuok Group's edible oil assets into Wilmar in 2007
Co-founded Wilmar International together in 1991 with Martua Sitorus, a Chinese Indonesian businessman
Father; arrived in British Malaya from Fuzhou, set up provision store in Mersing, later merged into Robert Kuok's Kuok Group
Second son; CEO of Caldecott Music Group, Co-Founder of BandLab Technologies, former owner of Rolling Stone magazine

family_members.name: Kuok Meng Ru
family_members.industry_or_organization: CEO of Caldecott Music Group; Co-Founder of BandLab Technologies

major_achievements.achievement: Co-founded Wilmar International with S$2 million in seed capital; grew it into world's largest palm oil trader and one of Asia's leading agribusiness groups
Merged Kuok Group's edible oil, trading and oil palm plantation assets into Wilmar after 16-year estrangement with uncle Robert Kuok, receiving 31% stake
Built Yihai Kerry into China's leading edible oil brand; listed subsidiary on Chinese stock exchange
Received the Chinese Government Friendship Award, PRC's highest award for foreigners who have made outstanding contributions to the country's economic and social progress
Built Wilmar into S$21 billion market cap company with over 300 subsidiaries and 92,000 employees
Received approval to redevelop 1 Undershaft in the City of London to match The Shard's height

biography_summary: Kuok Khoon Hong (born 1949, Mersing, Johor) is a Singaporean business magnate, entrepreneur and philanthropist, known as the 'Palm Oil King'. He earned a BBA from the National University of Singapore in 1973 and started trading soft commodities at Kuok Oils and Grains. In 1991, he co-founded Wilmar International with Martua Sitorus using S$2 million in seed capital. Under his leadership, Wilmar became the world's largest palm oil trader with S$21 billion market cap, 300+ subsidiaries, and 92,000 employees. He is also chairman of Yihai Kerry (China's leading edible oil brand) and non-executive chairman of Adani Wilmar. His estimated net worth is US$3.7 billion. He received the Chinese Government Friendship Award in 2019. He is the nephew of Robert Kuok and his second son Kuok Meng Ru founded BandLab Technologies.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with detailed career history, family members (father, mother, uncle Robert Kuok, sons Meng Han and Meng Ru), philanthropy details, birth place (Mersing, Johor), and major business achievements. Added relationships with Martua Sitorus and Robert Kuok.
